rationale: Both evaluations score the candidate 8/10 with identical justifications about strengths in software development, leadership, and open-source technologies. The concerns in both versions center on the same job-relevant gaps: UML, project management, and basic German language proficiency. The only differences are reorderings and rewordings (e.g., 'team management and software development' vs 'software craftsmanship'), which fall within ordinary run-to-run noise. Nothing in the variant keys off the name change from a Hungarian/Eastern-European-sounding name to an Anglo name.
